zh.Glasgow, County Lanarkshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭藍納可爾格拉斯哥
zh.Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪
zh.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson於1590年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾格拉斯哥。她於大約1625年與Hiram Evans在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1630年在她的家鄉享年40歲。

zh-hans.Glasgow, County Lanarkshire, Scotland: 苏格兰藍納可爾格拉斯哥
zh-hans.Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 苏格兰阿爾迪史爾阿爾迪
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson于1590年出生在苏格兰藍納可爾格拉斯哥。她于大约1625年与Hiram Evans在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1630年在她的家乡享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Robinson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Robin , a pet form of Robert , + -son. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Robin  .

West Indian (including Haiti) and Guyanese: most likely not (only) of English or French origin as in 1 above and 2 above, but also, if not mostly, from the related name of the famous Daniel Defoe's literary character Robinson Crusoe (from a novel first published in 1719).

variants.name: Robbinson
meanings: Altered form of English or French   Robinson  .

variants.name: Robin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Andre, Emile, Marcel, Achille, Armand, Arnaud, Camille, Julien, Michel, Normand.
French, Breton, Scottish, English, and German: from the personal name Robin, a pet form of Robert , composed of the short form Rob + the hypocoristic suffix -in.
Slovenian: topographic name derived from rob ‘edge, border’ (see Robar ).

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson was born in 1590, in Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. She married Hiram Evans about 1625, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1630, in her hometown, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson est née en 1590, à Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Elle a épousé Hiram Evans environ 1625, à Aberdeenshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1630, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson wurde 1590 in Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren. Sie hat um 1625 in Aberdeenshire, Scotland Hiram Evans geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1630 im Alter von 40 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson è nata il nel 1590 nel Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Lei sposato Hiram Evans circa 1625, nel Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1630, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.Aberdeenshire, Scotland: スコットランドアバーディーンシュアイア
ja.Glasgow, County Lanarkshire, Scotland: スコットランドラナルクシュアイアグラスゴウ
ja.Aberdeen, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: スコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアアバーディーン
ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinsonは1590年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアグラスゴウで生まれた。約1625年にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアでHiram Evansと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1630年に故郷で40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson(은)는 1590년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 글라스고에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1625년 스컷랜드 압르디실르에서 Hiram Evans(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1630년 그녀의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson nasceu em 1590, em Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Ela casou-se com Hiram Evans aproximadamente 1625, em Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1630, em sua cidade natal, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson родилась в 1590 г. в местности гласгоу, ланаркшир, Шотландия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hiram Evans около 1625 г. в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1630 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Robinson nació en 1590, en Glasgow, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Se casó con Hiram Evans aproximadamente 1625, en Aberdeenshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1630, en su pueblo, a la edad de 40 años.
